newsArray.title: IPL 2026: BCCI breaks silence on Riyan Parag vape video, seeks clarification from RR skipper
newsArray.description: The BCCI has responded to the viral vape video of Riyan Parag, seeking an explanation from the Rajasthan Royals captain. The incident, which surfaced during an IPL match, has sparked controversy as smoking and vaping are restricted inside stadium premises.
creator: chankesh rao

newsArray.title: WATCH: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s Epic Reply To ‘AI Chip’ Claims After IPL 2026 Blitz
newsArray.description:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi AI Chip Reply: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has been one of the standout performers in IPL 2026, and his blistering form has even sparked an unusual discussion off the field. Recently, Pakistani cricket analyst Nauman Niaz jokingly suggested that the youngster's bat might be fitted with an “AI chip” to explain his extraordinary hitting. The lighthearted remark quickly gained traction online. As his performances continued to grab headlines, curiosity grew around whether the youngster would respond to the viral claim. The Rajasthan Royals (RR) camp has now addressed the matter in a social media video, where Sooryavanshi was directly asked about the so-called “AI chip” in his bat. Here's what he said:

Bat pe AI chip hai kya tumhare?

Vaibhav answers 😂💗 pic.twitter.com/uZcqABbaGS

— Rajasthan Royals (@rajasthanroyals) April 29, 2026

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi Responds With Humour

Staying true to his calm and cheerful demeanor, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi delivered a witty reply that instantly won over fans.

"God has given it to me. He said himself that he has installed something in my bat. I am using that."

His response added a humorous twist to the ongoing buzz, while also reflecting the confidence and ease with which he has handled both on-field pressure and off-field attention.

Also Check: IPL 2026: Is Rohit Sharma Fit For MI vs SRH Clash? Here's What We Know

Another Explosive Knock vs PBKS

The conversation resurfaced after Sooryavanshi’s latest impactful outing against Punjab Kings (PBKS) on April 28. Chasing a stiff target, the youngster smashed a rapid 43 off just 16 deliveries, providing early momentum to his side.

Although it wasn’t his highest score of the season, the innings once again showcased his aggressive intent and ability to dominate bowlers from the outset. His contributions at the top have been instrumental in Rajasthan’s campaign so far.

Sooryavanshi’s consistency has been just as impressive as his strike rate. Across nine matches, he has amassed 400 runs at an average of 44.44 and a staggering strike rate of 238.10.

His tally includes one century and two half-centuries, placing him firmly at the top of the run charts. He currently holds the Orange Cap, staying ahead of established stars like Abhishek Sharma, Virat Kohli, and KL Rahul.
creator: suyash sahay

newsArray.title: Will Riyan Parag be Banned From IPL 2026? Viral Vaping Video Sparks Debate
newsArray.description: Riyan Parag to Be Banned for Two IPL 2026 Games for Vaping During PBKS-RR Match; BCCI Rule and Govt Act Under Spotlight Riyan Parag has landed in controversy after an incident during a Rajasthan Royals match against Punjab. The episode unfolded on Tuesday night when Parag, dismissed for a quick 29 off 16 balls, returned to the dugout. TV cameras zoomed in and captured him exhaling vapour from a vape while seated between teammates Yashasvi Jaiswal and Dhruv Jurel. The moment quickly drew attention, largely because it happened in full public view. Criticism has been sharp, with many fans pointing out that as captain, Parag is expected to set higher standards. The timing has added to the scrutiny, given his underwhelming form this season. He has managed just 117 runs in nine matches, averaging 14.62, numbers that raise serious concerns for a top-order leader. BCCI’s Incoming Ban Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) enforces strict stadium regulations, with smoking prohibited anywhere near the field or inside team areas. The IPL code says you can't bring the game into disrepute. Sources say a two-match ban is coming. This is the second time RR has been hit with a discipline fine lately. Their manager was already fined Rs. 1 lakh for using a phone. If Parag is suspended, Rajasthan loses their captain at the worst possible time. Illegal in India Here is the kicker: vaping is actually a crime in India. The PECA Act of 2019 bans these things entirely. You aren't allowed to sell them or even have them. For a first-time slip-up, you can technically go to jail for a year or pay a ₹1 lakh fine. Doing it on national TV is bold, and not in a good way. Parag is 24, so he’s old enough to know the law. What Now? The BCCI is checking the raw footage as we speak. The Anti-Corruption Unit is also looking at it. We haven't had a formal "you're out" yet, but it’s looking likely. RR will have to name a new captain for the next two games. Social media is already calling for his head. Parag needs to focus on his batting, not his vap.
creator: saheli majumder ambwani

newsArray.title: Massive 162,000 sq. ft. athletic complex in the works in northeast Calgary
newsArray.description: Northeast Calgary is getting a new place to play, with a massive 162,500 sq. ft. athletic complex in Saddle Ridge currently in the works. Located at the northwest corner of 80th Avenue NE and Metis Trail NE, the Northeast Athletic Complex will be one of the largest athletic parks in Calgary. It’s set to feature outdoor amenities such as four outdoor multi-use artificial turf fields and a competition-capable cricket field to host local, regional and national games, which will be the first of its kind in the city. Inside will feature a competition-sized indoor artificial turf field, indoor cricket batting nets, 16 team rooms and four change rooms for officials, multi-purpose, media, administration and maintenance rooms, washrooms, and spectator seating. The facility will also include over 500 parking spots and amenities such as pathways, green spaces, picnic areas, and a playground on the 35-acre space. The project is currently in the design and permitting phase. According to the City’s website, funds for the project were allocated in the City’s 2026 budget, meaning the facility is fully funded for construction. GEC Architecture was hired in the summer of 2025 to complete the design of the complex, and with the design process well underway, development permits have officially been submitted. While no construction or opening date is currently set, the City is working to finalize the project schedule and is beginning the process of selecting a contractor to complete the build. This is just one of many new developments on Calgary’s radar. Calgary YMCA and Calgary Public Library recently announced a 69,000 sq. ft recreation centre and library proposed for west Calgary, and two new high-rise apartments are in the works near CF Chinook Centre. You can learn more about the complex online.
creator: kendall collins

newsArray.title: Will Riyan Parag be Banned From IPL 2026? Viral Vaping Video Sparks Debate
newsArray.description: Riyan Parag to Be Banned for Two IPL 2026 Games for Vaping During PBKS-RR Match; BCCI Rule and Govt Act Under Spotlight Riyan Parag has landed in controversy after an incident during a Rajasthan Royals match against Punjab. The episode unfolded on Tuesday night when Parag, dismissed for a quick 29 off 16 balls, returned to the dugout. TV cameras zoomed in and captured him exhaling vapour from a vape while seated between teammates Yashasvi Jaiswal and Dhruv Jurel. The moment quickly drew attention, largely because it happened in full public view. Criticism has been sharp, with many fans pointing out that as captain, Parag is expected to set higher standards. The timing has added to the scrutiny, given his underwhelming form this season. He has managed just 117 runs in nine matches, averaging 14.62, numbers that raise serious concerns for a top-order leader. BCCI’s Incoming Ban Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) enforces strict stadium regulations, with smoking prohibited anywhere near the field or inside team areas. The IPL code says you can't bring the game into disrepute. Sources say a two-match ban is coming. This is the second time RR has been hit with a discipline fine lately. Their manager was already fined Rs. 1 lakh for using a phone. If Parag is suspended, Rajasthan loses their captain at the worst possible time. Illegal in India Here is the kicker: vaping is actually a crime in India. The PECA Act of 2019 bans these things entirely. You aren't allowed to sell them or even have them. For a first-time slip-up, you can technically go to jail for a year or pay a ₹1 lakh fine. Doing it on national TV is bold, and not in a good way. Parag is 24, so he’s old enough to know the law. What Now? The BCCI is checking the raw footage as we speak. The Anti-Corruption Unit is also looking at it. We haven't had a formal "you're out" yet, but it’s looking likely. RR will have to name a new captain for the next two games. Social media is already calling for his head. Parag needs to focus on his batting, not his vap.
creator: saheli majumder ambwani

newsArray.title: Massive 162,000 sq. ft. athletic complex in the works in northeast Calgary
newsArray.description: Northeast Calgary is getting a new place to play, with a massive 162,500 sq. ft. athletic complex in Saddle Ridge currently in the works. Located at the northwest corner of 80th Avenue NE and Metis Trail NE, the Northeast Athletic Complex will be one of the largest athletic parks in Calgary. It’s set to feature outdoor amenities such as four outdoor multi-use artificial turf fields and a competition-capable cricket field to host local, regional and national games, which will be the first of its kind in the city. Inside will feature a competition-sized indoor artificial turf field, indoor cricket batting nets, 16 team rooms and four change rooms for officials, multi-purpose, media, administration and maintenance rooms, washrooms, and spectator seating. The facility will also include over 500 parking spots and amenities such as pathways, green spaces, picnic areas, and a playground on the 35-acre space. The project is currently in the design and permitting phase. According to the City’s website, funds for the project were allocated in the City’s 2026 budget, meaning the facility is fully funded for construction. GEC Architecture was hired in the summer of 2025 to complete the design of the complex, and with the design process well underway, development permits have officially been submitted. While no construction or opening date is currently set, the City is working to finalize the project schedule and is beginning the process of selecting a contractor to complete the build. This is just one of many new developments on Calgary’s radar. Calgary YMCA and Calgary Public Library recently announced a 69,000 sq. ft recreation centre and library proposed for west Calgary, and two new high-rise apartments are in the works near CF Chinook Centre. You can learn more about the complex online.
creator: kendall collins
